
● Update(home/config/fish/app_aliases/nix/__main__.fish)
  ⎿  Added 25 lines
       1  alias n="nix"
       2  complete -c n -w nix
       3 +
       4 +# `nix develop`: fully offline when the env is already realized locally; online only
       5 +# when something is genuinely missing. The probe realizes the same env, so the offline
       6 +# develop after it is instant. --max-jobs 0 makes the probe fail fast instead of
       7 +# silently source-building GC'd deps. A genuine eval error gets evaluated twice (cheap).
       8 +# See ongoing_debug/2026-07-11_nix-develop-direnv-offline.md
       9 +function nix --wraps nix
      10 +  if test "$argv[1]" = develop
      11 +    # --command/-c consumes everything after it; strip for the probe
      12 +    set -l probe_args
      13 +    for a in $argv[2..]
      14 +      if contains -- $a --command -c
      15 +        break
      16 +      end
      17 +      set -a probe_args $a
      18 +    end
      19 +    if command nix print-dev-env --offline --max-jobs 0 $probe_args &>/dev/null
      20 +      command nix develop --offline $argv[2..]
      21 +    else
      22 +      command nix $argv
      23 +    end
      24 +  else
      25 +    command nix $argv
      26 +  end
      27 +end
      28  alias flake-build="sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#myhost --show-trace -L -v"
      29  #TODO!: make into a function and git-commit with $argv concatenation for message
      30  alias nixup="git -C '$NIXOS_CONFIG' add -A && nix flake update --flake '$NIXOS_CONFIG' && sudo nixos-rebuild switch --show-trace -v --impure --fast && git_upload '$NIXOS_CONFIG'"

  Thought for 6s (ctrl+o to expand)

● Now update dirr to use the designed force-reload path (nuking .direnv also nukes gcroots):

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

● Update(home/config/fish/app_aliases/direnv.fish)
  ⎿  Added 11 lines, removed 1 line
       7    direnv deny
       8  end
       9  complete -c dirw -w direnv
      10 -alias dirr="rm -r .direnv; dira" # for `direnv reload`
      10 +# manual reload: with global _nix_direnv_manual_reload this is THE way to update an env.
      11 +# nix-direnv-reload force-rebuilds in place (keeps gcroots); nuking .direnv is the fallback.
      12 +function dirr
      13 +  git add -A
      14 +  if test -x .direnv/bin/nix-direnv-reload
      15 +    .direnv/bin/nix-direnv-reload
      16 +  else
      17 +    rm -rf .direnv
      18 +    direnv allow
      19 +  end
      20 +end
      21  complete -c dirr -w direnv
      22  alias dird="direnv deny"
      23  complete -c dird -w direnv

● Now nix.settings hardening in configuration.nix:

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

● Update(os/nixos/configuration.nix)
  ⎿  Added 5 lines
      13    nix.settings = {
      14      experimental-features = [ "nix-command" "flakes" ];
      15      accept-flake-config = true;
      16 +    # never re-check cached tarballs / the global flake registry (default: hourly);
      17 +    # dep updates are manual (`nup`). See ongoing_debug/2026-07-11_nix-develop-direnv-offline.md
      18 +    tarball-ttl = 4294967295;
      19 +    # GC keeps the build closure of gcrooted dev shells, so direnv caches stay valid offline
      20 +    keep-outputs = true;
      21      trusted-substituters = [
      22        "https://cache.nixos.org"
      23        "https://nix-community.cachix.org"

  Thought for 23s (ctrl+o to expand)
